questions.subject: IOQM
questions.q:
Let $R$ denote the circular region in the $x y$-plane bounded by the circle $x^2+y^2=36$. The lines $x=4$ and $y=3$ divide $R$ into four regions $R_i, i=1,2,3$, 4 . If $\left|R_i\right|$ denotes the area of the region $R_i$ and if $\left|\mathrm{R}_1\right|>\left|\mathrm{R}_2\right|>\left|\mathrm{R}_3\right|>\left|\mathrm{R}_4\right|$, determine $\left|\mathrm{R}_1\right|-\left|\mathrm{R}_2\right|-\left|\mathrm{R}_3\right|+\left|\mathrm{R}_4\right|$.
[Here $|\Omega|$ denotes the area of the region $\Omega$ in the plane.]
questions.solution: $R_1=\int_{-2 \sqrt{5}}^3 \sqrt{36-y^2} d y=\frac{1}{2} y \sqrt{36-y^2}+\frac{1}{2}(36) \sin ^{-1} \frac{y}{6}$ Similarly for using integration. we can find $\begin{gathered} \mathrm{R}_1+\mathrm{R}_4=\frac{1}{2} \pi(6)^2+2(3)(4) \\ \therefore \text { Remaining circle area }=\mathrm{R}_2+\mathrm{R}_3=\frac{1}{2} \pi(6)^2-2(3)(4) \end{gathered}$ Hence, $R_1+R_4-\left(R_2+R_3\right)=24+24=48$
